name: tk-agent-team
description: Opinionated Claude Code Agent Teams plugin with durable per-agent YAML memory, a curator skill, and extensible domain specialist teammates.

display.vibe: Teams that remember — every task makes the team smarter

agents.name: reviewer
agents.description: Three-persona code review family sharing a memory namespace

agents.name: developer
agents.description: Two-persona implementation family — reads reviewer memory to pre-apply known complaints before building

agents.name: curator
agents.description: Memory health manager — runs the curation pipeline on demand

agents.name: orchestrator
agents.description: Routing dispatcher — classifies prompts, pre-loads memory, hands off to the right skill

agents.name: planner
agents.description: Two-persona planning family — product requirements and technical design

agents.name: researcher
agents.description: Read-only codebase archaeologist producing context briefs

agents.name: tester
agents.description: Two-persona testing family — unit coverage and integration scenarios

agents.name: debugger
agents.description: Root-cause investigator — reproduces failures and hypothesizes causes, hands off to developer for fix

agents.name: docs-writer
agents.description: Technical writer authoring READMEs, API docs, and /compound solution records

skills.name: memory-curate
skills.description: Use when an agent's memory file exceeds the soft or hard limit — runs dedupe, score-and-drop, summarize stages to shrink under the soft limit.

skills.name: ideate
skills.description: Use when the user asks to explore options or brainstorm directions for a fuzzy problem — produces 3–5 ranked scored ideas with tradeoffs and a recommendation.

skills.name: brainstorm
skills.description: Use when the user has selected an idea and needs it expanded into user stories and Given/When/Then acceptance criteria.

skills.name: plan
skills.description: Use when requirements exist or the user asks for a technical design, ADR, or implementation plan — produces a plan doc reviewed for standing-decision conflicts.

skills.name: work
skills.description: Use when an approved plan doc exists and the user wants code written — classifies frontend/backend/full-stack and dispatches developer personas in parallel.

skills.name: review
skills.description: Use when code has been written and needs multi-lens critique — runs architecture, correctness, and security reviewers in parallel and merges findings.

skills.name: test
skills.description: Use when new code needs test coverage — runs unit and integration testers in parallel, writes test files, and produces a merged coverage-gap report.

skills.name: debug
skills.description: Use when a bug, stack trace, or regression is reported — runs researcher → debugger → reviewer → developer and writes a durable bug-fix solution doc.

skills.name: ship
skills.description: Use when the user wants a plan taken to implemented+reviewed+tested in one shot — composite skill running /work → /review → /test with halt-on-blocker.

skills.name: compound
skills.description: Use at the end of a full cycle to harvest the run into durable signal — writes a solution doc and curates every family memory touched during the cycle.
